changeSet.author: claude
changeSet.sql: CREATE TABLE plugin_itam_log ( id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() CONSTRAINT plugin_itam_log_pr_key PRIMARY KEY, customerId INT NOT NULL, deviceId INT NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id), ownerName VARCHAR(250), ownershipDate TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, assetStatus VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, deviceCondition VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL, batteryCondition VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL, comments TEXT, pictures TEXT, -- JSON-encoded array of local file paths, max 5 items (enforced at app layer) loggedByUserId INT REFERENCES users(id), deletedByUserId INT REFERENCES users(id), createdAt TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(), deletedAt TIMESTAMPTZ, CONSTRAINT plugin_itam_log_asset_status_chk CHECK (assetStatus IN ('IN_USE', 'IN_STOCK', 'UNDER_REPAIR', 'RETIRED')), CONSTRAINT plugin_itam_log_device_condition_chk CHECK (deviceCondition IN ('GOOD', 'BAD')), CONSTRAINT plugin_itam_log_battery_condition_chk CHECK (batteryCondition IN ('GOOD', 'BAD')) ); CREATE INDEX plugin_itam_log_device_idx ON plugin_itam_log (deviceId); CREATE INDEX plugin_itam_log_created_idx ON plugin_itam_log (createdAt DESC); CREATE INDEX plugin_itam_log_deleted_idx ON plugin_itam_log (deletedAt);

changeSet.author: claude
changeSet.sql: INSERT INTO plugins ( identifier, name, description, javascriptModuleFile, functionsViewTemplate, settingsViewTemplate, namelocalizationkey, settingsPermission, functionsPermission, deviceFunctionsPermission ) VALUES ( 'itam', 'IT Asset Management', 'Tracks device ownership, condition and status history', 'app/components/plugins/itam/itam.module.js', 'app/components/plugins/itam/views/itam.html', null, 'plugin.itam.localization.key.name', 'plugin_itam_view', 'plugin_itam_view', null );

changeSet.author: claude
changeSet.comment: Permissions for itam plugin: view, create, delete, export
changeSet.rollback: DELETE FROM permissions WHERE name IN ('plugin_itam_view', 'plugin_itam_create', 'plugin_itam_delete', 'plugin_itam_export');

changeSet.author: claude
changeSet.comment: Restrict itam permissions to admin only: revoke the explicit grants from every role. The super-admin role bypasses permission checks entirely (SecurityContext.hasPermission / auth.service.js), so the admin login keeps full access with no grant; every other role loses it by default and can be granted access again via the Roles admin screen.

changeSet.author: claude
changeSet.comment: Promote itam from the Functions dropdown to a top-level tab: nulling functionsViewTemplate removes it from the Functions menu (built from plugins with a non-null functionsViewTemplate), while the plugin stays registered so its JS module still lazy-loads and its REST API stays live. The top-level tab is wired in content.html / app.js / tabs.controller.js.
changeSet.sql: UPDATE plugins SET functionsViewTemplate = NULL WHERE identifier = 'itam';
changeSet.rollback: UPDATE plugins SET functionsViewTemplate = 'app/components/plugins/itam/views/itam.html' WHERE identifier = 'itam';

changeSet.author: claude
changeSet.comment: Grant the itam permissions to the org-admin (Administrator) role as well. The super-admin role already has implicit access; this makes the Administrator role (roleId 2, the app's fixed role.orgadmin.id per build.properties, and the convention used for org-admin grants throughout db.changelog.xml) an admin that keeps ITAM by default. All other roles stay revoked from the previous changeset.

changeSet.author: claude
changeSet.comment: Make ITAM logs survive a hard device delete. deviceId's FK to devices(id) had no ON DELETE clause, so Postgres defaulted to NO ACTION/RESTRICT: deleting a device from the Devices view would fail outright with a foreign-key violation as soon as that device had any ITAM log rows. deviceNumber was already a domain/mapper field, but only ever populated by a LEFT JOIN to devices at read time -- the join goes blank the moment the device row is gone. Persisting deviceNumber directly on each row at creation time (backfilled here for existing rows) and dropping the FK entirely gives ITAM logs a permanent, self-contained reference: device deletion can never be blocked by ITAM data, and old entries keep displaying their device number correctly even after that device row -- and any device row that later re-enrolls under the same number -- comes and goes. The FK's auto-generated name is looked up dynamically rather than hardcoded, since Postgres's default naming isn't guaranteed across installs that may have created the table slightly differently. splitStatements="false" is required here: Liquibase's own statement-splitter breaks on every literal ";" before the SQL ever reaches Postgres, including the ones inside the DO $$ ... $$ block below (e.g. "fk_name text;"), which cuts the dollar-quoted block in half and fails app startup with "Unterminated dollar quote". Disabling the splitter sends this whole block as one string over the wire, which Postgres's own simple-query protocol executes correctly as a sequence of semicolon-separated statements.
